attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Look at some keys by the light of a tall lamp.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts an indoor room with wooden flooring and warm-toned walls. The primary focus is on a rectangular glass-top dining table surrounded by four wicker-backed chairs with wooden frames. On the table, there are several objects:

1. Objects on the Table:
   - A black laptop computer is open and placed near the center-right of the table. It is not a receptacle and is not defined as any type in the domain file.
   - Three golden statuettes, each mounted on a black rectangular base, are arranged on the table. These are decorative objects and are not receptacles.
   - A small red rectangular object, possibly a card or a small book, lies near the center of the table. It is not a receptacle.
   - A set of metallic keys is visible near the front-left corner of the table, resting on the glass surface. The keys are small, metallic, and appear to be a standard house key ring. They are not receptacles.

2. Receptacles and Other Objects in the Scene:
   - A tall table lamp with a large, conical, cream-colored fabric shade and a dark brown base is positioned on a side table or console to the left of the scene (visible in the second image). The lamp is not a receptacle but is a toggleable object (it can be turned on/off). It is currently illuminated, casting a warm glow.
   - In the background (second image), a dark brown media console or cabinet is visible, with a flat-screen television on top. The cabinet may be a receptacle, but no openable doors or drawers are visible, and it is not specified in the domain as a receptacle type.
   - No microwave, fridge, sink, or knife is visible in any of the images. Therefore, none of the domain-defined receptacles (microwave, fridge, sink) are present in the scene.

3. Spatial Relationships and States:
   - The keys are located on the glass table surface, near the front-left corner, adjacent to one of the wicker chairs.
   - The lamp is located on a separate piece of furniture (side table) to the left of the main table. It is not on the table itself.
   - The lamp is currently turned on (isOn is true), as indicated by the illuminated shade and the warm light it casts on surrounding surfaces.
   - The robot is not present in the scene, and no agent is holding any object. The keys are not being held by anyone and are not in any receptacle.
   - The laptop, statuettes, and red card are all resting on the table surface, not in any receptacle.
   - The chairs are positioned around the table, with one directly in front of the viewer (bottom of the image) and others on the left, right, and far side.

4. Object Properties:
   - The keys are metallic and have a standard ring with multiple key blades. They are small and lie flat on the table.
   - The lamp has a dark brown base and a cream-colored, textured lampshade with a slightly tapered shape. It is a tall lamp, as described in the instruction.
   - The table has a transparent glass top, allowing the wooden floor to be visible beneath it.
   - The chairs have light brown wicker backs and seats with dark wooden frames.

5. Relevance to Instruction:
   - The instruction is to “Look at some keys by the light of a tall lamp.” The keys are on the table, and the tall lamp is on a nearby side table, illuminating the scene. The lamp is currently on, providing light. The keys are not being held or moved; they are stationary on the table surface.

Note: Since no domain-defined receptacles (microwave, fridge, sink) or knives are present, and the keys and lamp are not defined as domain objects (except the lamp can be toggled), the only relevant predicates for planning would be:
- atLocation (for the robot to go to the keys or the lamp)
- isOn (for the lamp, which is currently true)
- holds (if the robot picks up the keys)

The scene does not contain any objects that require cleaning, heating, cooling, slicing, or being placed in a receptacle as per the domain definitions. The primary task is to position the agent near the keys under the lamp’s light.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem look_at_keys_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        keys - object
        tall_lamp - object
        dining_table - object
        side_table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; The robot is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot keys))
        (not (atLocation robot tall_lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot dining_table))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))

        ; The keys are on the dining table
        (atLocation keys dining_table)

        ; The tall lamp is on the side table
        (atLocation tall_lamp side_table)

        ; The lamp is currently on (as per scene description)
        (isOn tall_lamp)

        ; The robot is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; No objects are in receptacles (none defined in scene)
        ; No cleaning, heating, cooling, slicing needed
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The robot must be at the location of the keys to look at them
            (atLocation robot keys)
            ; The lamp must be on to provide light (it already is, but we ensure it remains on)
            (isOn tall_lamp)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem look_at_keys_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        keys - object
        tall_lamp - object
        dining_table - object
        side_table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; The robot is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot keys))
        (not (atLocation robot tall_lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot dining_table))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))

        ; The keys are on the dining table
        (atLocation keys dining_table)

        ; The tall lamp is on the side table
        (atLocation tall_lamp side_table)

        ; The lamp is currently on (as per scene description)
        (isOn tall_lamp)

        ; The robot is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; No objects are in receptacles (none defined in scene)
        ; No cleaning, heating, cooling, slicing needed
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The robot must be at the location of the keys to look at them
            (atLocation robot keys)
            ; The lamp must be on to provide light (it already is, but we ensure it remains on)
            (isOn tall_lamp)
        )
    )
)

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts an indoor environment with wooden flooring and warm ambient lighting. The primary focus is on a dining area and a nearby side table with a lamp.

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - Receptacles: There are no objects in the scene that match the domain-defined receptacle types (microwave, fridge, sink). The table and chairs are not classified as receptacles in this domain.
   - Objects: The following objects are present and relevant to the domain:
     - Laptop: A black laptop with a visible screen and keyboard, resting on the glass-top dining table.
     - Red Card: A small red rectangular object (possibly a card or tag) placed on the table near the laptop.
     - Golden Statues: Three golden humanoid statues on black square bases, positioned on the table.
     - Keys: A small metallic ring-shaped object (keys) is visible on the table, near one of the statues.
     - Lamp: A tall table lamp with a dark brown base and a large, cream-colored, textured lampshade. It is placed on a side table or dresser.
     - Television: A flat-screen TV is visible in the background on a dark cabinet, but it is not a receptacle or object type defined in the domain.
     - Chairs: Four wicker-backed chairs with wooden frames surround the table. They are not receptacles in this domain.

2. Quantities:
   - Laptop: 1
   - Red Card: 1
   - Golden Statues: 3
   - Keys: 1 (as a single set/ring)
   - Lamp: 1
   - Television: 1
   - Chairs: 4

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The laptop, red card, three golden statues, and keys are all placed on the glass-top dining table.
   - The keys are located near the front-left golden statue (from the viewer’s perspective).
   - The lamp is on a separate side table or dresser, positioned to the left side of the scene, adjacent to a wall.
   - The television is in the background, on a cabinet, behind and to the right of the lamp.
   - The chairs are arranged around the dining table, with one chair directly in front of the viewer and others on the sides.

4. Current State of Objects (Relevant to Planning Task):
   - The laptop is closed and not being held or toggled.
   - The red card is stationary on the table.
   - The golden statues are stationary on their bases.
   - The keys are stationary on the table.
   - The lamp is not currently toggled on (no visible light emission in the first and third images; however, the second image shows a close-up with the lampshade illuminated, suggesting it can be toggled on).
   - No objects are held by any agent (the robot is not present in the scene).
   - No objects are in receptacles (since no receptacles are defined or present in the domain context).
   - No objects are clean, hot, cool, or sliced.

5. Colors and Visual Properties:
   - Laptop: Black casing, dark screen, gray trackpad.
   - Red Card: Bright red, rectangular, small size.
   - Golden Statues: Metallic gold finish, human-like figures in dynamic poses, mounted on matte black square bases.
   - Keys: Metallic silver or gray, ring-shaped, small and flat.
   - Lamp: Dark brown base, cream-colored lampshade with a woven or textured pattern.
   - Table: Transparent glass top with a wooden frame.
   - Chairs: Light brown wicker backs and seats, dark brown wooden frames.
   - Floor: Polished wooden planks with warm brown tones.
   - Walls: Light beige or tan color.
   - Television: Black bezel, dark screen.

Note: The instruction “Look at some keys by the light of a tall lamp” implies that the lamp must be toggled on to illuminate the keys. The keys are on the dining table, while the lamp is on a separate side table. The robot must navigate to the lamp, toggle it on, and then position itself to view the keys under its light. No agent (robot) is currently present or at any location in the scene.

(define (problem look_at_keys_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        laptop - object
        red_card - object
        golden_statue1 - object
        golden_statue2 - object
        golden_statue3 - object
        keys - object
        lamp - object
        dining_table - object
        side_table - object
        chair1 - object
        chair2 - object
        chair3 - object
        chair4 - object
        television - object
    )
    (:init
        ; The robot is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot dining_table))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))
        (not (atLocation robot lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot keys))

        ; Objects are on the dining table
        (atLocation dining_table laptop)
        (atLocation dining_table red_card)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue1)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue2)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue3)
        (atLocation dining_table keys)

        ; Lamp is on the side table
        (atLocation side_table lamp)

        ; Television is on a cabinet (not relevant for receptacle or action)
        (atLocation television)

        ; Chairs are around the table (not relevant for receptacle or action)
        (atLocation chair1)
        (atLocation chair2)
        (atLocation chair3)
        (atLocation chair4)

        ; Lamp is not toggled on initially
        (not (isOn lamp))

        ; No objects are held
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; No objects are in receptacles (none defined)
        ; No objects are clean, hot, cool, or sliced
    )

        (and
            ; The lamp must be toggled on to provide light

)

(define (problem look_at_keys_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        laptop - object
        red_card - object
        golden_statue1 - object
        golden_statue2 - object
        golden_statue3 - object
        keys - object
        lamp - object
        dining_table - object
        side_table - object
        chair1 - object
        chair2 - object
        chair3 - object
        chair4 - object
        television - object
    )
    (:init
        ; The robot is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot dining_table))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))
        (not (atLocation robot lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot keys))

        ; Objects are on the dining table
        (atLocation dining_table laptop)
        (atLocation dining_table red_card)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue1)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue2)
        (atLocation dining_table golden_statue3)
        (atLocation dining_table keys)

        ; Lamp is on the side table
        (atLocation side_table lamp)

        ; Television is on a cabinet (not relevant for receptacle or action)
        (atLocation television)

        ; Chairs are around the table (not relevant for receptacle or action)
        (atLocation chair1)
        (atLocation chair2)
        (atLocation chair3)
        (atLocation chair4)

        ; Lamp is not toggled on initially
        (not (isOn lamp))

        ; No objects are held
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; No objects are in receptacles (none defined)
        ; No objects are clean, hot, cool, or sliced
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The lamp must be toggled on to provide light
            (isOn lamp)
            ; The robot must be at the location of the keys to look at them
            (atLocation robot keys)
        )
    )
)

 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
